perturbation.original_text: Each party agrees that it will take all reasonable steps to protect the secrecy of and avoid disclosure or use of Confidential Information of the other party on order to prevent of unauthorized falling into the public domain or the possession of unauthorized persons.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party agrees that it may take steps to protect the secrecy of and avoid disclosure or use of Confidential Information of the other party on order to prevent of unauthorized falling into the public domain or the possession of unauthorized persons.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'will take all reasonable steps' to 'may take steps' weakens the obligation to protect confidential information. This creates an in-text contradiction because the agreement is built on the assumption of strong confidentiality, but now protection is discretionary. This is located in section 3.
perturbation.contradicted_text: General Provisions. This Agreement will be governed by the ------------------ laws of the State of California. This Agreement will be binding upon the successors of each party, and will be for the benefit of each party, its successors, and its assigns. Each party agrees that it would be difficult to measure the damage to such party from the breach of the other party's obligations hereunder, that injury to such party from any such breach would be impossible to calculate, and that monetary damages would therefor be an inadequate remedy; accordingly, each party agrees that the other party shall be entitled, in addition to all other remedies it might have, to injunctions or other appropriate orders to restrain any such breach without showing or proving any actual damage.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'will take all reasonable steps' to 'may take steps' weakens the obligation to protect confidential information. The contradicted text states "Each party agrees that it would be difficult to measure the damage to such party from the breach of the other party's obligations hereunder...", and that 'the other party shall be entitled, in addition to all other remedies it might have, to injunctions or other appropriate orders to restrain any such breach without showing or proving any actual damage.' By making the protection of confidential information discretionary, the change directly conflicts with the assurance of strong protection implied in the contradicted text, creating uncertainty about the obligations of each party, leading to a strong in-text contradiction. If the protection is optional, it is not assured that steps will be taken. The right to injunctions is therefore contradicted since it is no longer assured that steps will be taken.

perturbation.original_text: Each party will have employees to whom Confidential Information of the other party is disclosed sign a Non-Disclosure Agreement in content substantially similar to this agreement if such persons have not already signed such agreements obligating them to hold the Confidential Information in confidence.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party will encourage employees to whom Confidential Information of the other party is disclosed to sign a Non-Disclosure Agreement in content substantially similar to this agreement if such persons have not already signed such agreements obligating them to hold the Confidential Information in confidence.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'will have employees sign' to 'will encourage employees to sign' weakens the requirement for employees to sign NDAs. This introduces a contradiction because the original text implies mandatory NDA signing, while the modified version suggests it's optional. Because it is not clearly said they *have* to sign the NDA, then this weakens the obligation of secrecy being held by both parties, which contradicts in section 3.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Non-Disclosure of Confidential Information. Each party agrees ------------------------------------------ not to use the Confidential Information disclosed to it by the other party for its own use or for any purpose except to carry out discussions concerning the completion of any business relationship between the two. Each party will not disclose the Confidential Information of the other party to third parties or to its employees except employees who are required to have the information in order to carry out the contemplated business. Each party will have employees to whom Confidential Information of the other party is disclosed sign a Non-Disclosure Agreement in content substantially similar to this agreement if such persons have not already signed such agreements obligating them to hold the Confidential Information in confidence. Each party agrees that it will take all reasonable steps to protect the secrecy of and avoid disclosure or use of Confidential Information of the other party on order to prevent of unauthorized falling into the public domain or the possession of unauthorized persons. Each agrees to immediately notify the other party in writing of any misuse or misappropriation of such Confidential Information of the other party which may come to its attention.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated "Each party will have employees to whom Confidential Information of the other party is disclosed sign a Non-Disclosure Agreement", creating an obligation for employees to sign the NDA. The changed text modifies this to "Each party will encourage employees to whom Confidential Information of the other party is disclosed to sign a Non-Disclosure Agreement", weakening the obligation to simply encouragement. This introduces a contradiction because the original text implies mandatory NDA signing, while the modified version suggests it's optional. The contradicted text reinforces the original obligation to ensure Non-Disclosure. The weakening of this obligation causes a direct conflict, leading to a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Upon request of the disclosing party, --------------------- the receiving party agrees to promptly return all documents furnishes to it by the disclosing party, together with all copies thereof in its possession.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon request of the disclosing party, --------------------- the receiving party will attempt to return all documents furnishes to it by the disclosing party, together with all copies thereof in its possession.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'agrees to promptly return' to 'will attempt to return' introduces a contradiction by weakening the obligation to return confidential documents. 'Agrees to' creates a contractual obligation while 'will attempt to' introduces uncertainty about the return of materials, which is inconsistent with the agreement's confidentiality goals. This directly contradicts section 3
perturbation.contradicted_text: Non-Disclosure of Confidential Information. Each party agrees ------------------------------------------ not to use the Confidential Information disclosed to it by the other party for its own use or for any purpose except to carry out discussions concerning the completion of any business relationship between the two. Each party will not disclose the Confidential Information of the other party to third parties or to its employees except employees who are required to have the information in order to carry out the contemplated business. Each party will have employees to whom Confidential Information of the other party is disclosed sign a Non-Disclosure Agreement in content substantially similar to this agreement if such persons have not already signed such agreements obligating them to hold the Confidential Information in confidence. Each party agrees that it will take all reasonable steps to protect the secrecy of and avoid disclosure or use of Confidential Information of the other party on order to prevent of unauthorized falling into the public domain or the possession of unauthorized persons. Each agrees to immediately notify the other party in writing of any misuse or misappropriation of such Confidential Information of the other party which may come to its attention.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'agrees to promptly return' to 'will attempt to return' weakens the obligation to return confidential documents. The original phrase created a clear contractual duty, while the changed phrase introduces uncertainty. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text,' which outlines a strong obligation to protect and not disclose confidential information. The contradicted text emphasizes taking 'all reasonable steps to protect the secrecy' of confidential information and 'immediately notify' the other party of any misuse. Changing the language to 'attempt' directly undermines the explicit agreement not to disclose information. The change creates a contradiction based on the provided explanation and fits the 'Misaligned Terminology' definition where the changed term introduces conflict with other terms in the contract.

perturbation.original_text: Each agrees to immediately notify the other party in writing of any misuse or misappropriation of such Confidential Information of the other party which may come to its attention.
perturbation.changed_text: Each will consider notifying the other party in writing of any misuse or misappropriation of such Confidential Information of the other party which may come to its attention.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'agrees to immediately notify' to 'will consider notifying' weakens the obligation to report any misuse or misappropriation of confidential information. This change contradicts the purpose of the NDA, which is to protect sensitive information, because immediate notification is critical. Therefore, there is a contradiction with the original purposes as defined in section 1.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Purpose. Company and SoloPoint wish to explore a business ------- possibility under which each party may disclose its Confidential Information to the other party.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates immediate notification of misuse of confidential information. The changed text weakens this obligation to 'will consider notifying', creating a significant inconsistency. The contradicted text states that the purpose of the agreement is to explore a business possibility where each party may disclose confidential information to the other, implying that there is an intent to protect that confidential information by ensuring notification. Changing 'agrees to immediately notify' to 'will consider notifying' directly contradicts the purpose of the agreement because immediate notification is important in protecting confidential information. Therefore, there is a contradiction with the original purposes as defined in the provided contradicted text, as stated in the explanation. The type of perturbation is 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' which states 'Misaligned terminology also leads to in-text contradictions when the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.'
